channel.title: Boston Women in Bioinformatics's Events
channel.description: Stay updated with our upcoming events and meetups

item.title: CIC's Open Work & Collaborative Days: Women in Tech
item.content:encoded:
When: Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 12:00 PM

Where: CIC Cambridge, One Broadway, 5th Floor

Are you looking to connect with others in your field in a fresh, dynamic, and innovative environment? Join us for CIC's Open Work & Collaborative Days, your opportunity to explore our office and event spaces, experience our vibrant energy and ecosystem, meet members of our incredible team, and access exclusive programming and meaningful connections.

This particular session is designed specifically for women in technology, creating space for connection, collaboration, and community among women across the tech ecosystem. We're hoping to bring together founders, engineers, product leaders, investors, creatives, and technologists for a day of coworking, organic networking, and intentional programming.

Hours: 8AM–4PM, followed by Venture Café Thursday Gathering from 4:30–8PM.

item.title: Single Cell Basics: A 2-Day Hands-On scRNA-seq Workshop for Beginner
item.content:encoded:
When: Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 5:00 PM

Where: Zoom

Have you read single-cell RNA-seq papers but never run the analysis yourself? This beginner-friendly workshop will take you from raw 10x Genomics data to identifying real biological cell types.

Hosted by Boston Women in Bioinformatics (BWiB) and sponsored by Sprout Informatics, this 2-day live Zoom series is designed for students and early-stage researchers ready to get hands-on with single-cell analysis.

What you'll do:

  • Understand how single-cell RNA-seq experiments work
  • Load a real PBMC dataset from 10x Genomics
  • Perform quality control (UMIs, gene counts, mitochondrial %)
  • Normalize and cluster cells
  • Identify marker genes
  • Determine the most abundant cell type — and explain why

What will we provide:

  • A dedicated cloud environment with all required packages pre-installed and data pre-downloaded
  • Instruction on how to connect via VS Code and run the analysis in a Jupyter notebook
  • Git repo with markdown files and step-by-step instructions

Day 1 — April 2

Understanding the experiment, connecting it to the gene-by-cell count matrix, setting up the analysis environment, and performing QC and filtering.

Day 2 — April 3

From clean data to biological insight: normalization, dimensionality reduction (PCA/UMAP), clustering, marker gene analysis, and assigning cell identities.

By the end of the workshop, you will confidently interpret a UMAP and defend your biological conclusion.

Who is this for?

  • Beginners to scRNA-seq
  • Students looking for structured, hands-on training
  • Researchers who want to move from theory to practice

Summary

  • Format: Live on Zoom
  • Registration: $5 — all proceeds go directly to support BWIB!
  • Spots may be limited due to compute capacity

item.title: Boston Women in Bioinformatics Networking Session at Festival of Genomics
item.content:encoded:
When: Thursday, June 4, 2026 at 3:30 PM

Where: Genome Dome - Boston Convention and Exhibition Center (BCEC), Boston, MA

Boston Women in Bioinformatics is returning to the Genome Dome (yes, the inflatable igloo) at the Festival of Genomics with a structured speed networking event. Join us Thursday, June 4 from 11:30 AM to 1:00 PM for fast-paced, one-on-one conversations with women working across bioinformatics, research, and industry.

Walk away with one memorable detail about every person you meet. Not just a LinkedIn connection, but a real reason to follow up. The goal isn't a bigger network. It's a better one: peer support, shared commiseration, and the occasional "I know exactly who you should talk to."

Pro tip: bring something to write with. One memorable detail per person goes a long way and you are going to want to write it down!

↓ Download the event flyer (PDF)
